arsenal.title: Mr. Champions League
arsenal.fact: All-time top scorer (140 goals) and assist provider (42 assists) in UEFA Champions League history, holding the record for most knockout stage goals.

arsenal.title: International Goal King
arsenal.fact: The highest scoring international player of all time with 130+ goals for Portugal, captaining them to Euro 2016 and Nations League success.

arsenal.title: Athletic Longevity
arsenal.fact: Has scored 400+ goals since turning 30 years old, proving unparalleled work ethic, physical dominance, and adaptation across 4 different leagues.

arsenal.title: Clutch Performer
arsenal.fact: Holds the record for the most match-winning goals in Champions League history and has scored in 3 separate UCL finals.

counterattacks.claim: Messi has a superior goals-and-assists-per-game ratio in the Champions League. Ronaldo has more goals simply because he has played more games.
counterattacks.defenseUpgrade: Point out that Ronaldo's dominance shows up when it matters most: in the quarter-finals, semi-finals, and finals, where he has more goals than any other player by a huge margin.

counterattacks.claim: Ronaldo's international goal record is padded by weak European opposition in qualifiers, whereas Messi won the actual FIFA World Cup against top-tier South American and European nations.
counterattacks.defenseUpgrade: Highlight Ronaldo's Euro 2016 victory (Portugal's first-ever major trophy) and the competitive nature of the European Championships, where he is also the all-time top scorer.

counterattacks.claim: Longevity doesn't beat peak talent. Messi's 91 goals in 2012 and his playmaking genius make him a superior footballer, whereas Ronaldo is just a supreme finisher.
counterattacks.defenseUpgrade: Explain that Ronaldo is not just a finisher; he started as a dynamic winger with elite dribbling and crossing stats before adapting his game to become the ultimate goalscorer, showing superior complete athleticism.

strengths: Excellent focus on Ronaldo's UCL knockout statistics and unmatched clutch credentials.
Good point regarding his international record and longevity over 4 top-tier leagues.
weaknesses: Allowed the playmaker argument to go unchallenged.
Did not address Messi's higher number of Ballon d'Ors.
missedOpportunities: You forgot to mention Ronaldo's 3 consecutive UCL titles with Real Madrid.
Could have cited Ronaldo's weak-foot and header statistics to highlight his complete physical scoring package.
coachFeedback.upgradeSuggestion: Reinforce that Ronaldo has succeeded in multiple distinct leagues (Premier League, La Liga, Serie A) under different tactical systems, whereas Messi spent his prime in a single Barcelona system.

round1.comment: Strong physical and stats arguments, but vulnerable to playmaker and World Cup comparisons.

round2.comment: Phenomenal rebuttal. Using the 'multiple leagues' argument and highlighting the high ratio of UCL knockout goals completely re-balanced the debate.
